prompt: Implement a backend rule so archived projects cannot receive new tasks; start with the right first protection and keep validation focused.
expected_qualities: Chooses the Backend route explicitly and explains why.
Uses a failing behavior test or equivalent backend-first protection before implementation.
Describes the owning boundary and the smallest change to make the rule pass.
Keeps validation limited to targeted checks tied to the changed rule.
assert_against: Issue-definition-style output with scope/non-goals/task-slice framing as the main deliverable.
Jumping to broad test suites or unrelated audits without risk-based justification.

prompt: Build the new empty, loading, and error states for the notifications panel with accessible messaging and keyboard-safe retry behavior.
expected_qualities: Chooses the Frontend route explicitly and explains why.
Starts from visible states or behavior scenarios rather than backend-style TDD language.
Names the component or screen boundary and the smallest implementation step.
Includes focused validation for the affected UI states and accessibility behavior.
assert_against: Routing this as backend or fullstack without justification.
Turning the response into a final merge-readiness report.

prompt: Implement the invite-teammate flow across the settings UI and POST /workspace-invites API in the smallest vertical slice.
expected_qualities: Chooses the Fullstack route explicitly and identifies the seam or contract first.
Protects both sides of the seam instead of treating the change as a loose bundle of tasks.
Keeps the change incremental and names a smallest vertical slice.
Uses targeted seam-relevant validation rather than broad exploratory checks.
assert_against: Ignoring the UI/API boundary and routing purely as frontend or backend when both are clearly in scope.
Expanding into issue-framing content such as Definition of Ready or broad acceptance-criteria drafting.

prompt: Fix a regression where deleting the last address in checkout leaves the page stuck on a spinner; reproduce first and protect against reintroducing it.
expected_qualities: Chooses the Bugfix route explicitly and explains the reproduce-first approach.
Uses a repro or regression protection as the first step before broader cleanup.
Keeps the implementation to the smallest change that resolves the stuck-spinner behavior.
Limits validation to the regression path and closely related checkout behavior.
assert_against: Treating the bug as a generic refactor or using a broad audit workflow.
Skipping regression protection in favor of only manual confidence.

prompt: Refactor the permissions resolver to reduce duplication while preserving current role checks and response shapes.
expected_qualities: Chooses the Refactor route explicitly and defines the preserved-behavior boundary.
Adds characterization or equivalent protection before structural changes.
Focuses on smallest safe restructuring steps instead of redesigning the subsystem.
Keeps validation centered on unchanged role-check behavior and affected contracts.
assert_against: Introducing new feature scope under the refactor label.
Escalating to a full architecture review instead of a focused implementation workflow.
